Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Player (green) moved UP again to rows 41-43. The bottom-center charcoal box (id=7) has been collected/disappeared! Goal is to collect all 3 blue-centered boxes. 2 remain: top-right [24-27, 44-47] and left [28-31, 16-19].
- Plan: Navigate to remaining boxes. Go UP then RIGHT to reach the top-right box first.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Wait, I need to reconsider. The UP action had no impact - the player didn't move. But looking at the state before the UP, I see:
- id=7: color=G (dark gray), rows=[36-39], cols=[32-35] - this is the bottom-center box that's still there!
- id=8: color=b (blue), rows=[37-38], cols=[33-34] - blue center inside it
So the box wasn't collected! The player just moved past it. The player is at rows 41-43, which is below the box at 36-39. The player can't move UP further because the box is blocking the way.
Wait, but the player moved from 45-47 to 41-43 on the second UP. That means the player jumped over the box? No, the box is at 36-39 and the player ended at 41-43. The player stopped just below the box.
So the boxes are obstacles, not collectibles. Let me reconsider what the goal is.
Actually wait - let me look more carefully. The box at [36-39, 32-35] is still there (id=7, color=G). But the player is at [41-43, 32-35]. The player can't move UP because the box is in the way.
Let me reconsider the goal. Maybe I need to navigate around obstacles to reach certain positions, or maybe I need to use SPACE to interact with something.
Let me try RIGHT to move sideways and see if I can navigate around the obstacles.
Actually, let me reconsider what happened. The first two UP actions moved the player from 49-51 to 45-47 to 41-43. The third UP had no impact because the box at 36-39 is blocking.
So the layout is:
- Player: 41-43, 32-35
- Box below player was collected (originally at 36-39) - wait no, it's still there as id=7
Hmm, but the box is at 36-39 and player at 41-43. The player is BELOW the box. So when I try to go UP, the player can't pass through the box.
Wait, I'm confused about the coordinate system. Row 0 is at the top, row 63 is at the bottom. So:
- The box at rows 36-39 is ABOVE the player at rows 41-43
- Going UP means going to lower row numbers
So the player is blocked from going UP by the box at 36-39.
Let me try going RIGHT or LEFT to navigate around.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole situation. The player started at 49-51. After first UP, moved to 45-47. After second UP, moved to 41-43. Third UP blocked.
The box at 36-39 is directly above the player. I need to go around it.
Let me try RIGHT to move sideways.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at rows 41-43, cols 32-35. UP is blocked by the box at 36-39. The bottom-center box was NOT collected - it's still there (id=7). Need to navigate around obstacles. Goal still unclear - need to explore more.
Plan: Try RIGHT to navigate around the blocking box.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at 41-43, 32-35. UP blocked by box at 36-39.
# Try RIGHT to go around
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"RIGHT result: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Green at rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")

# Also check what's around
print("\nAll non-background objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] == 0: continue  # skip background
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows=[{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols=[{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
